hero.title: What goes wrong with natural and Afro hair
hero.standfirst: What each one looks like, what usually causes it, and the latest pieces on each.

sections.title: My hair feels dry no matter what I try
sections.body: This is most often one of five things — and the float test you've been told to use isn't reliable for figuring out which. Hard water buildup, low porosity, protein-moisture imbalance, and seasonal humidity loss are the usual suspects. The fix depends on which.

related_articles.title: Why Is My 4C Hair Always Dry? (The UK-Specific Answer)
Hard Water and Curly Hair UK: What's Actually Wrecking Your Curls
Why Does Natural Hair Feel Dry Right After Washing?

sections.title: My hair isn't growing
sections.body: Almost always retention, not growth. Your hair is growing — at roughly the same rate as everyone else's. What's happening is breakage faster than the growth at the root can keep up. The fix is mechanical, not metabolic.

related_articles.title: Why Won't My 4C Hair Grow? (A UK Answer)
Breakage vs Shedding Natural Hair: How to Tell the Difference
Protective Styles and Length Retention: What Actually Works

sections.title: My products stopped working
sections.body: Usually water, season, or porosity shift. Moving house can switch you from soft to hard water overnight. Autumn drops indoor humidity below the threshold where glycerin works. Chemical processing changes the cuticle. Same routine, different result.

related_articles.title: Why Your Natural Hair Products Stopped Working
The Porosity Test Is Broken — Here's What Actually Matters
Glycerin in Natural Hair Products: Summer vs Winter in the UK

sections.title: My child's curls and I don't know where to start
sections.body: No judgement. Whether or not you share your child's texture, the foundations are the same — water, detangling, protective styling, products that aren't actively damaging. We start where you are.

related_articles.title: How to Look After Mixed Race Children's Hair: The Honest UK Starter Guide
White Parent, Black Child Hair: Where to Actually Start
Wash Day Routine for Mixed Race Children UK: Step by Step

sections.title: My scalp is itchy, flaky, or sore
sections.body: Some scalp symptoms are routine — buildup, infrequent washing, a new product irritating you. Some are medical and need a GP. CCCA, seborrhoeic dermatitis, and folliculitis all need clinical attention, not deep conditioner. We help you tell which is which.

related_articles.title: Scalp Care for Natural Hair UK: The Part Most Routines Skip
Scalp Itchy After Washing Natural Hair
Thinning Edges on Natural Hair UK: Causes, Recovery, and When to See a Trichologist

catchall.body: These are the most common ones, but they aren't the only ones. Browse the full hair-care library to find what you're looking for.
